StyledText.Data: IO Settings in Title <<FAT Title::Reference title>>

Calculation.Text: "View all blocks in this Title that can use this IO type. "
"View all blocks in this Title that can use this IO type. "

StyledText.Data: This IO Type requires a Title list for the different options. The IO stores a value from this list.. When displaying the reult from the IO input, this list is used to fetch other data like texts, media or similar. For Tag filter usage The IO selected for here is also used for all Tags when a list is used as a Tag filter. If using this IO as a Tag filter selection - make sure you assign the correct list of tags to this IO!

Calculation.Text: "Select list with IO group for this IO"

StyledText.Data: IO List is used as a special concept for grouping numerical values (i.e IO Rate) from multiple IOs into a new IO. It is only used for reading, not writing purposes. The selected "IO list" is a list of one or multiple IO values with a numerical value for each list item. This list type is also called "IO List". With the IO type "IO List" it is possible to show average result and deviation from multiple IO Rates that belongs to a specific group or category and show a sorted version of this list. Example: IO List item 1 = "Fish" ( IO Rate 1 = "Cod", IO Rate 2 = "Pike", IO Rate 3 = "Salmon") IO List item 2 = "Meat" (IO Rate 4 = "Pig", IO Rate 5 = "Cow", IO Rate 6 = "Lamb") In this way we can sort the list of Fish and Meat, with the highest rate value on all sub categories on top.
List with possible values to store in IO

Texts connected to the IO are needed if the IO data will be displayed together with data from other IOs and sorted in a dynamic order. (For example in a sorted list with multiple IO Rates and sorted with highest value on top. ) In a case where the IO result will be displayed in a dynamic order, the texts describing what's stored in the IO cannot be authored in a static way.

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "list_item_id" ; FAT List item @ List @ IO::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "caller" ; "Title List" )

LayoutObject.name: T1 LT

Calculation.Text: FAT Block @ Page item::Label override List Text 1
Length ( Self ) > BlockTemplate @ Page item::List Text 1 max characters And BlockTemplate @ Page item::List Text 1 max characters>0
FAT List item @ List @ Page item::Include in export = 0

LayoutObject.name: T1 LT

Calculation.Text: FAT Block @ Page item::Label override List Text 1
Length ( Self ) > BlockTemplate @ Page item::List Text 1 max characters And BlockTemplate @ Page item::List Text 1 max characters>0
FAT List item @ List @ Page item::Include in export = 0
IO Type::Number of text fields < 1 Or FAT Title::gViewTranslatedLanguage
IO Type::Number of text fields < 1 Or Not FAT Title::gViewTranslatedLanguage
IO Type::Number of text fields < 2 Or FAT Title::gViewTranslatedLanguage
IO Type::Number of text fields < 2 Or Not FAT Title::gViewTranslatedLanguage
"Enter a description of the usage of the IO"
"Enter a descriptive name for the IO"

StyledText.Data: Name your IO with an understandable name. It makes it easier when you connect your Page item to the IO when authoring.

Selecting IO type will determine which kind of building blocks that can use the IO when authoring and when displaying result (Insights - on Funkis Platform only). Some examples: Action - IO Create Action Input, IO Show Action Output Comment - IO Comment Input, IO Comment Output Choice - IO Multiple Choice Input, IO Dilemma Input, IO Multiple Choice Output Note - IO Postit Input, IO Postit Output, IO Postit Categorize, IO Rate - IO Rate Input, IO Rate Output Media - IO Media Upload Multiple lists - IO Lists Input, IO List Output Quiz - IO Quiz Input Universal - Can be used with any block but result cannot be analysed in Insights.

ParagraphStyle.Text: Action - IO Create Action Input, IO Show Action Output Comment - IO Comment Input, IO Comment Output Choice - IO Multiple Choice Input, IO Dilemma Input, IO Multiple Choice Output Note - IO Postit Input, IO Postit Output, IO Postit Categorize, IO Rate - IO Rate Input, IO Rate Output Media - IO Media Upload Multiple lists - IO Lists Input, IO List Output Quiz - IO Quiz Input Universal - Can be used with any block but result cannot be analysed in Insights.

Calculation.Text: Not IO Type::xAPI enabled /*not PatternCount ( FAT Title::Backend ; "xAPI" ) or not PatternCount ( FAT Title::Delivery format ; "XAPI" ) or */

StyledText.Data: NOTE! This choice is only valid if your Title's backend = xAPI or if your selected delivery format = XAPI. This choice decides if the user data managed by this IO should be sent to the Learning Record Store, LRS. If IO data can be interesting to track and monitor, set this choice to Yes.
xAPI reporting not enabled for this IO type

Calculation.Text: "Select the input context for the IO"
"Select the input context for the IO"

StyledText.Data: The input context indicates how this IO is used the first time in the title. Or who writes to this IO? There are three differents contexts for the input: Member - it is a member who give input to this IO. Another member cannot change this input. Group - it is a group who gives input. This means any member can change the input and then it will be changed for all members in the group. Session - it is a session who gives input. This means any member or group in the session can change the input and it will be chsnged for all members in the session. This context is mainly used for a facilitator who wants to configure or communicate with all members in the group.

Calculation.Text: "Select all who are allowed to read the saved data in this IO"

StyledText.Data: Check all permissions that you would like to use for this IO in the title. My Member The current user is allowed to read inputs from the IO. My Group The current user's group and all participants in the group are allowed to read inputs from the IO. My Session The current user's session and all participants and groups in the session are allowed to read inputs from the IO.

CharacterStyle.Text: Check all permissions that you would like to use for this IO in the title.

Calculation.Text: "Select all who are allowed to write to this IO"

StyledText.Data: My Member The current user is allowed to write and edit inputs in the current IO. My Group The current user's group and other users in the group are allowed to write and edit inputs in the current IO. My Session The current user's session, as well as users and groups in a session, are allowed to write and edit inputs in the current IO.
Modifiers are not available for this IO type.
IO modifiers and input fields for <<Name>>
Input type: <<FAT Modifier @ IO::Type>> <<FAT Modifier @ IO::Description>>

LayoutObject.name: Media filename

Calculation.Text: FAT Modifier @ IO::Type ≠ "Media" Or Not SubtitleMedia ( Media MV IO::Container file extension )

LayoutObject.name: Color FM
Text Input value
String Number Value

Calculation.Text: FAT Modifier @ IO::Type ≠ "String" And FAT Modifier @ IO::Type ≠ "Prettified string" And FAT Modifier @ IO::Type ≠ "URL encoded string" And FAT Modifier @ IO::Type ≠ "Number" And FAT Modifier @ IO::Type ≠ "Sync server path"
FAT Modifier @ IO::Type ≠ "Value list radio" Or ValueCount ( FAT Modifier @ IO::ValuesList) > 4
FAT Modifier @ IO::Type ≠ "Value list radio" Or ValueCount ( FAT Modifier @ IO::ValuesList) < 5
SetProperty ( "io_id" ; FAT IO::PrimaryKey) & SetProperty ( "title_id" ; FAT IO::Title id ) & SetProperty ( "new_io" ; False ) & SetProperty ( "tab" ; "settings" )
"Create a new text field to this IO"

LayoutObject.name: Portal

Calculation.Text: IO Field Type @ IO::Value type = "IO Field"
IO Field Type @ IO::Value type = "IO Field"
IO Field Type @ IO::Value type = "IO Field"

LayoutObject.name: String Number Value 2 Copy3
String Number Value 2 Copy4
String Number Value 2 Copy8
String Number Value 2 Copy3
String Number Value 2 Copy4
String Number Value 2 Copy8

Calculation.Text: IO Field Type @ IO::Value type = "IO Field"
SetProperty ( "numberOfChildrenField"; GetFieldName ( FAT IO::Number of fields )) & SetProperty ( "sortField"; GetFieldName ( FAT IO Field @ IO::SortIndex )) & SetProperty ( "action"; "up")
SetProperty ( "numberOfChildrenField"; GetFieldName ( FAT IO::Number of fields )) & SetProperty ( "sortField"; GetFieldName ( FAT IO Field @ IO::SortIndex )) & SetProperty ( "action"; "down")

ScriptReference.name: Fix missing text fields for IO field

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "field_id" ; FAT IO Field @ IO::PrimaryKey) & SetProperty ( "title_id" ; FAT IO::Title id ) & SetProperty ( "tab" ; "settings" )
"Create a new text field to this IO"
IO Type::IO has fields = 0 Or IO Field Type @ IO::Value type = "IO Field"

SetProperty ( "field_id" ; FAT IO Field @ IO::PrimaryKey) & SetProperty ( "list_name" ; FAT IO Field @ IO::Name) & SetProperty ( "list_type" ; "field") & SetProperty ( "title_id" ; FAT IO::Title id ) & SetProperty ( "list_template_id" ; "BAF6DADF-7209-4916-BD32-980F27F248EA" ) // Multiple choice
"Create a new text field to this IO"
IO Field Type @ IO::Value type ≠ "List" Or Not IsEmpty ( FAT IO Field @ IO::List id )
IO Field Type @ IO::Value type ≠ "IO Field"
IO Field Type @ IO::Value type ≠ "IO Field"

StyledText.Data: <<ƒ:Case ( FAT IO Field @ IO::Number of Linked fields > 0 ; "SHARED"; "" )>>

An IO with input fields creates an expremely powerful solution for managing user input. Each input field can be of a specific type and have a unique configuration. This offers a variety possibilities from simple post-it notes to more complex forms like a task. Type - Each input field must have a type that decides what the user can fill in to the field. Text - Displays a text input field that can be defined with: Minimum and maximum number of chars allowed to be entered by the user. Leave blank for no limitations. Number of rows sets the number of rows of the input field at start. Popup menu - Displays a popup menu with a list that is created in the title. Date picker Displays a popup calendar picker. Name - The name of the input field. Only available for FAT authors. Label input - A text that will be displayed above the input field. Leave blank if no label needed. Only visible in IO input blocks. The text will be translated. Label output - A text that will be displayed above the field when displaying what's entered. Leave blank if no label needed. The text will be translated. Hint text - A text that will guide or hint users what to input in the input field. Leave blank if no hint needed. The text will be translated. Input required - Decides if input is required for the field or not. If input is required, the post IO will not be saved until there's user input in the field.

CharacterStyle.Text: Each input field must have a type that decides what the user can fill in to the field.

- Displays a text input field that can be defined with: Minimum and maximum number of chars allowed to be entered by the user. Leave blank for no limitations. Number of rows sets the number of rows of the input field at start.
- Displays a popup menu with a list that is created in the title.
- A text that will be displayed above the input field. Leave blank if no label needed. Only visible in IO input blocks. The text will be translated.
- A text that will be displayed above the field when displaying what's entered.
Leave blank if no label needed.
- A text that will guide or hint users what to input in the input field.
Leave blank if no hint needed.
- Decides if input is required for the field or not. If input is required, the post IO will not be saved until there's user input in the field.

Calculation.Text: "Enter a name for your text variable"
"Paste text variable string that you will use in the title"
FAT Text variable @ IO::IO Output ≠ "Index" Or FAT Text variable @ IO::IO Context = "Title"
"Enter a name for your text variable"
"Enter a default text when text variable is not set."
IsEmpty ( TF TV IO Default text::PrimaryKey )

StyledText.Data: Set a default text when empty

Calculation.Text: Not IsEmpty ( TF TV IO Default text::PrimaryKey )

StyledText.Data: Set a default text when empty

Calculation.Text: IsEmpty ( TF TV IO Default text::PrimaryKey )
FAT IO::IO type = "Select" Or FAT IO::IO type = "Rate"
FAT IO::IO type = "Select" Or FAT IO::IO type = "Rate"
SetProperty ( "type" ; "Text variable" ) & SetProperty ( "parent_id" ; FAT Text variable @ IO::PrimaryKey )
IsEmpty ( FAT Text variable @ IO::Group by IO )

ValueListReference.name: IO Fields in IO Grouped by

Calculation.Text: FAT IO @ Text variable Grouped by::IO type ≠ "post"
FAT IO @ Text variable Grouped by::IO type ≠ "select" And FAT IO @ Text variable Grouped by::IO type ≠ "rate"
FAT IO @ Text variable Grouped by::IO type ≠ "post" And FAT IO @ Text variable Grouped by::IO type ≠ "select" And FAT IO @ Text variable Grouped by::IO type ≠ "rate"
FAT IO @ Text variable Grouped by::IO type ≠ "post" And FAT IO @ Text variable Grouped by::IO type ≠ "select" And FAT IO @ Text variable Grouped by::IO type ≠ "rate"
FAT IO @ Page item::IO type & ": " & FAT IO @ Page item::Author description

StyledText.Data: A Text variable can be created by combining data from one IO with another IO and present the result in a table. This can be useful when designing an AI prompt or if displaying input result from votes, choices or free-text aspects.

Calculation.Text: "Create a text variable table by combining two IOs."

StyledText.Data: Use dropdowns to create the text variable you want to use. Select IO data Some IO types have more than one data field to read from. Select data from popop menu. IO Context Select if text variable data belongs to member, group or session. Member: Text variable will display IO input from the current member only. Group: Text variable will display IO input that belongs to the group. Session: Text variable will display IO input that belongs to the session. Auto: Funkis Player will display member input in scoll lackages and group input in slide packages. IO Output Decide how you want the text variable to be displayed. Do you want only one saved item or multiple? Index if you want just one saved item. Enter which index number Click pen-icon to view the output or to add a manually created text variable (not recommended).

CharacterStyle.Text: : Text variable will display IO input from the current member only.
: Text variable will display IO input that belongs to the group.
: Text variable will display IO input that belongs to the session.
: Funkis Player will display member input in scoll lackages and group input in slide packages.

StyledText.Data: Create a text variable by choosing options from dropdown menus if you want to display text input from this IO as an ordinary text in any block

Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "referer_id" ; FAT IO::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "title_id" ; FAT IO::Title id ) & SetProperty ( "referer_type" ; "io" )

PopoverPanel.name: Input field settings popover

Calculation.Text: //not FAT IO Value @ Page item::IO Array
FAT IO Field @ IO Condition @ TV @ IO::Value type = "List"

ValueListReference.name: IO Fields List item @ TV VL

Calculation.Text: FAT IO Field @ IO Condition @ TV @ IO::Value type ≠ "List"
FAT IO Field @ IO Condition @ TV @ IO::Exposed Value type = "Text" Or FAT IO Field @ IO Condition @ TV @ IO::Exposed Value type = "Dropdown"

TableOccurrenceReference.name: FAT IO Field @ IO Condition @ TV @ IO

Calculation.Text: FAT IO Field @ IO Condition @ TV @ IO::Exposed Value type ≠ "Text" And FAT IO Field @ IO Condition @ TV @ IO::Exposed Value type ≠ "Dropdown"

StyledText.Data: Filter for which saved IOs to show

Calculation.Text: "Select an input field to add a condition"

StyledText.Data: Show only input where input field

Calculation.Text: //not FAT IO Value @ Page item::IO Array

TableOccurrenceReference.name: FAT IO Field @ IO SortOrder @ IO

Calculation.Text: "Select an input field to sort inputs"
"Click to make IO invisible in Insights"
"Click to make IO visible in Insights"

StyledText.Data: This IO is not monitored by Funkis Insight. Click on eye to include.
This IO is monitored by Funkis Insight. Click on eye to exclude.

Calculation.Text: "Enter a description when analysing result from this IO in the Insights module."
Not PatternCount ( FAT Title::Title Backend ; "Funkis Cloud" ) Or IO Type::List for options = 0
Not PatternCount ( FAT Title::Title Backend ; "Funkis Cloud" ) Or IO Type::List for options=0

StyledText.Data: Insight filter is only valid for Titles using IO data stored on Funkis Cloud. A filter is always based on a list selection. Two IOs cannot use the same filter. The filter can be used to slice the data in Insight.

Calculation.Text: Not PatternCount ( FAT Title::Title Backend ; "Funkis Cloud" ) Or IO Type::List for options=0

LayoutObject.name: Portal 2

Calculation.Text: IO Field Type @ IO::Value type = "IO Field"

SetProperty ( "field_id" ; FAT IO Field @ IO::PrimaryKey) & SetProperty ( "list_name" ; FAT IO Field @ IO::Name) & SetProperty ( "list_type" ; "aspect") & SetProperty ( "title_id" ; FAT IO::Title id ) & SetProperty ( "list_template_id" ; "86C0C3A5-ECEE-4BCC-93C8-ED40635609D9" ) // Aspect
"Create a new text field to this IO"
SetProperty ( "list_id" ; FAT IO Field @ IO::Aspect List id) & SetProperty ( "call_layout" ; Get(layoutName))
IsEmpty(FAT IO Field @ IO::Aspect List id)

StyledText.Data: Text variable information for this IO type. Can be used for manual text variable creation.

Create text variables that makes it easier to display text that is stored in this IO anywhere in the Title. Please check if the ioContext property in the { ioContext:auto} is correct. ioContext:auto - Funkis Player will display member input in scoll lackages and group input in slide packages. ioContext:member: Text variable will display IO input from the current member only. ioContext:group: Text variable will display IO input that belongs to the group. ioContext:session: Text variable will display IO input that belongs to the session.

Calculation.Text: "Select the input context for the IO"
"Select the input context for the IO"

StyledText.Data: The input context indicates how this IO is used the first time in the title. Or who writes to this IO? There are three differents contexts for the input: Member - it is a member who give input to this IO. Another member cannot change this input. Group - it is a group who gives input. This means any member can change the input and then it will be changed for all members in the group. Session - it is a session who gives input. This means any member or group in the session can change the input and it will be chsnged for all members in the session. This context is mainly used for a facilitator who wants to configure or communicate with all members in the group.

Title.Text: "Source data IO - Not for standard productions!"

StyledText.Data: Source data IO is an experimental feature that are used to workaround some limitations in FAT. It works currently only with the Sprig story IO. Source data IO is needed when using user input as a selection basis.

Calculation.Text: "Enter a prompt that will be sent together with the result when analysing stored data."
"View all page items using this IO"
